food_subgroup: Fried chicken with potato fries, spicy sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for loaded fries with chicken and sriracha sauce. FOODON_00002764 (french fries food product) used as closest parent. The chicken topping and sriracha sauce are not captured by this term.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP (Chicken meat, fried) used as closest parent for the chicken component. No composite FoodEx2 code exists for loaded fries with fried chicken and sriracha sauce as a single dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this composite fast food dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving of Chicken Loaded Fries Sriracha Flavour is approximately 280 g, consisting of a portion of deep-fried potato fries topped with seasoned fried chicken pieces and sriracha-flavoured sauce, as typically served at Jollibee Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken and French Fries composite — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, fried (NDB 05062) and Fast foods, potato, french fried in vegetable oil (NDB 21138); proportional blend estimated at 55% fries, 35% fried chicken, 10% sriracha sauce
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct nutritional entry exists for Jollibee Chicken Loaded Fries Sriracha Flavour in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, or any approved primary regional source. Values are estimated from a proportional blend of USDA SR Legacy entries: fast-food french fries (NDB 21138, ~55% by weight), fried chicken meat only (NDB 05062, ~35% by weight), and a sriracha-type hot sauce component (~10% by weight, estimated from USDA FoodData Central sriracha sauce data). All values are per 100 g of the composite dish as served.

gi_source.title: French fries GI reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. French fries (deep-fried potato) GI ~63–75; midpoint 65 used. Chicken component has negligible GI contribution.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chicken Loaded Fries Sriracha Flavour. GI of 65 is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component (deep-fried potato fries), which has a published GI range of 63–75 in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). The chicken and sriracha sauce components contribute negligible glycemic carbohydrates. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 71.7) / 100 ≈ 46.6, rounded to 46.5. Serving carbohydrate = 25.6 g/100g × 280 g serving = 71.7 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1176 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish as a single serving provides approximately 51% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, primarily from seasoning, sriracha sauce, and fried chicken coating.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ~47 per serving) and high refined carbohydrate content from deep-fried potato fries
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this dish as an occasional indulgence only, as the high glycemic load (~47 per serving) from deep-fried potato fries can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion control and pairing with low-GI foods are strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated total fat, saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and sodium from deep-frying and processed seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is high in total fat (12.8 g/100 g), saturated fat (2.4 g/100 g), and sodium (420 mg/100 g), and contains trace trans fat from deep-frying; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and choose lower-fat, lower-sodium alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Loaded Fries Sriracha Flavour is a fast food dish served at Jollibee Singapore, consisting of deep-fried potato fries topped with seasoned fried chicken pieces and a sriracha-flavoured spicy sauce. The dish is ultra-processed and prepared by deep-frying both the potato fries and the chicken in vegetable oil, then assembling with a commercially prepared sriracha-style hot sauce. The texture combines crispy, golden fries with tender, lightly battered fried chicken, and the sriracha sauce adds a tangy, moderately spicy heat profile. The dish is halal-certified at Jollibee Singapore and is popular as a snack or side meal. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 248 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content (~10 g/100 g) from the chicken, significant carbohydrates (~26 g/100 g) from the fries, and moderate fat (~13 g/100 g) from deep-frying. Sodium is notably high (~420 mg/100 g), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), driven by the deep-fried potato component, and the glycemic load per serving is high (~47), making it unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans due to the chicken content.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for Jollibee Chicken Loaded Fries Sriracha Flavour was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, or any approved primary regional source. Values are estimated from a proportional blend of USDA SR Legacy entries for deep-fried potato fries and fried chicken, with a sriracha sauce component. The GI is analogously estimated from published french fries GI data. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the component source entries and cannot be reliably estimated for this composite. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy source entries used. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the approved source data for this composite dish. The dish is served at Jollibee Bugis Village, Singapore, and is tagged as Halal. The food_group is retained as 'Meat and alternatives' from the input, consistent with the chicken protein component being the primary protein source. The sriracha flavour is a commercial sauce variant and not a traditional sriracha product; nutritional contribution is estimated. Glycemic load per serving calculated using 280 g serving size and 25.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g: GL = (65 × 71.7) / 100 ≈ 46.5.
